per_task.raw_excerpt:
Based on the LilyGO T-Display-S3 documentation provided in context:

- **I2C SCL**: GPIO17 (PIN_IIC_SCL)
- **I2C SDA**: GPIO18 (PIN_IIC_SDA)

Based on the injected LilyGO context for the T-Display-S3, here are the GPIO pins for the ST7789 LCD data lines (8-bit parallel bus):

| Data Line | GPIO |
|-----------|------|
| D0        | 39   |
| D1        | 40   |
| D2        | 41   |
| D3        | 42   |
| D4        | 45   |
| D5        | 46   |
| D6        | 47   |
| D7        | 48   |

Additionally, the control lines are:
- CS (Chip Select) = GPIO 6
- DC (Data/Command) = GPIO 7
- RST (Reset) = GPIO 5
- WR (Write) = GPIO 8
- RD (Read) = G

Based on the LilyGO context provided for the T-Display-S3, the LCD control line GPIOs are:

| Control Line | GPIO |
|---|---|
| **CS** (Chip Select) | GPIO6 |
| **DC** (Data/Command) | GPIO7 |
| **RST** (Reset) | GPIO5 |
| **WR** (Write) | GPIO8 |
| **RD** (Read) | GPIO9 |

These are the 8-bit parallel display bus control signals for the ST7789 display controller on the T-Display-S3.

Based on the LilyGO T-Display-S3 hardware configuration provided:

- **LCD backlight:** GPIO38 (PIN_LCD_BL)
- **Screen power:** GPIO15 (PIN_POWER_ON)

For your dark screen issue, you'll need to ensure both are properly initialized:

1. **Set GPIO15 high** to enable power to the display subsystem
2. **Set GPIO38 high** to turn on the backlight

If your screen is dark, check that:
- GPIO15 is being held HIGH during setup (enables the display power rail)
- GPIO38 is being set HIGH to enable the bac

According to the official LilyGO T-Display-S3 factory pin configuration, the two user buttons are:

- **BUTTON1 (BOOT button): GPIO0** — `PIN_BUTTON_1=GPIO0`
- **BUTTON2: GPIO14** — `PIN_BUTTON_2=GPIO14`

Source: [Official factory pin_config.h](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Display-S3/blob/master/examples/factory/pin_config.h) (lines 21–58)  
Hash: `sha256:3862678dd9d23971970d6952a6a5ee43d7f7df28b539c8fc5b26db784e846fa4`

**PIN_BAT_VOLT = GPIO4**

This is the ADC pin that reads the battery voltage divider on the T-Display-S3.

**Source:** Official factory `pin_config.h` (lines 21-58)  
**Authority:** V3-source-reference, exact confidence  
**Repository:** https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Display-S3/blob/master/examples/factory/pin_config.h  
**Hash:** sha256:3862678dd9d23971970d6952a6a5ee43d7f7df28b539c8fc5b26db784e846fa4

GPIO4 is the ADC input connected to the battery voltage divider circuit, allowing you to

Based on the official LilyGO source:

**SPI SCLK:** GPIO5  
**Radio CS/NSS:** GPIO18

Source: [LilyGo-LoRa-Series/examples/Factory/utilities.h](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGo-LoRa-Series/blob/master/examples/Factory/utilities.h) lines 89–141 (sha256:1f234017d45099f90e3a5cb7fb6ed16b78dfe11fa57f2b82a58f9d1b9411f733)

For reference, the complete LoRa SPI bus is:
- SCLK = GPIO5
- MOSI = GPIO27
- MISO = GPIO19
- CS = GPIO18
- RST = GPIO23
- DIO0 = GPIO26
- DIO1 = GPIO33
- BUSY = GPIO32

Based on the official LilyGO T-Beam documentation:

**GPS UART pins on T-Beam:**
- **GPS RX** (into ESP32): **GPIO34**
- **GPS TX** (from GPS module): **GPIO12**

The GPS UART also runs at **9600 baud**.

**Source:** LilyGO Factory utilities.h ([github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGo-LoRa-Series](https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGo-LoRa-Series/blob/master/examples/Factory/utilities.h), lines 89–141, SHA256: `1f234017d45099f90e3a5cb7fb6ed16b78dfe11fa57f2b82a58f9d1b9411f733`)

Based on the LilyGO T-Connect-Pro pinout information provided:

- **I2C SDA**: GPIO39 (IIC_SDA)
- **I2C SCL**: GPIO40 (IIC_SCL)

These are the standard I2C pins for the board's internal I2C bus.

On the T-Watch Ultra (CO5300 AMOLED on QSPI):

- **Display CS (Chip Select) = GPIO41**
- **Display SCK/CLK (Serial Clock) = GPIO40**

This is from the official T-Watch-Ultra pins definition:
**Source:** https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/LilyGoLib-PlatformIO/blob/master/variants/lilygo_twatch_ultra/pins_arduino.h (lines 15–71, sha256: 0a29c5d0dfcc6815718f751bcac04469fe9ced21483ba63fc68a0cb6cfe20568)

The full QSPI display bus uses:
- **CS=GPIO41, SCK=GPIO40, D0=GPIO38, D1=GPIO39, D2=GPIO42, D3=GP

Based on the LilyGO T-Deck specifications provided in the context, the keyboard interrupt (BOARD_KEYBOARD_INT) line is **GPIO46**.

This is the interrupt pin for the on-board I2C keyboard controller, which communicates via the I2C bus (SDA=GPIO18, SCL=GPIO8).

**GPIO10** must be driven high to power on the T-Deck board peripherals.

This is defined as `BOARD_POWERON=GPIO10` in the official T-Deck firmware:
- **Source:** https://github.com/Xinyuan-LilyGO/T-Deck/blob/master/examples/UnitTest/utilities.h (lines 1–95)
- **Hash:** sha256:3b2401b6b00f8a0aaa45e10864236488aebacf92b1fcd4cc1e7c8f02759f3c76
- **Authority rank:** 90 (exact match)

In your initialization code, set GPIO10 to `HIGH` before accessing other peripherals.
